content: I just got back from a 6-month sabbatical traveling through South America. What a trip!
Welcome back! That sounds incredible. Where did you go exactly?
Started in Peru, then through Bolivia, Chile, Argentina, and finished in Brazil. I'm Quinn, by the way, a geologist by trade.
Nice to meet you, Quinn! A geologist traveling through South America - that must have been fascinating from a professional perspective too.
Absolutely! I'm 51 and work in mining exploration. Seeing those landscapes up close was incredible for my work.
I can imagine! So what hobbies do you enjoy when you're not exploring mines or traveling?
I'm really into origami - I find it strangely relaxing after a day of analyzing rock samples. I also do archery and brew my own beer.
Origami, archery, and brewing beer? That's quite a unique combination! How did you get into origami?
A colleague at the mine introduced me to it during a long shift. Now I can make pretty complex designs. Oh, and I've been doing stand-up comedy as a hobby too.
Stand-up comedy? Now that's unexpected! How long have you been doing that?
About 3 years now. I started doing open mics at a local club. It's terrifying but addictive.
That takes real courage! Do you have a favorite topic you joke about?
Mostly geology puns and mining stories. You'd be surprised how many people don't know the difference between a rock and a mineral!
I can only imagine! So what's next for you after your South American adventure?
I'm actually thinking about starting a podcast about geology for non-geologists. Kind of like making my field accessible to everyone.
That sounds like a fantastic idea! What would you call it?
